neighborhoods.name: Presqu'ile (1er & 2e)
neighborhoods.description: The peninsula between the Rhone and Saone rivers, Lyon's commercial and cultural heart with Place Bellecour
neighborhoods.character: Dense urban, premier shopping and dining, UNESCO World Heritage

housing.predominantType: Haussmann-era apartments, often with high ceilings and parquet floors

neighborhoods.character_notes: Place Bellecour is Europe's largest pedestrian square. Lyon's famous bouchons (traditional restaurants) concentrated here.

neighborhoods.name: Vieux Lyon (5e)
neighborhoods.description: Medieval and Renaissance quarter at the foot of Fourviere hill, one of Europe's largest intact Renaissance neighborhoods
neighborhoods.character: Historic, touristic, narrow traboules (passageways), charming but compact
neighborhoods.character_notes: UNESCO World Heritage. Many buildings date to 15th-16th century. Can be noisy on weekends due to nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Monplaisir (8e)
neighborhoods.description: Authentic residential neighborhood in the 8th arrondissement, birthplace of cinema (Lumiere brothers), with local markets and village feel
neighborhoods.character: Residential village-within-a-city, local shops, quieter pace

housing.predominantType: Mix of older apartment buildings and newer residences

neighborhoods.character_notes: The Lumiere Institute and museum is here. Excellent daily market on Place Monplaisir. Metro D provides quick access to center.

neighborhoods.name: Caluire-et-Cuire
neighborhoods.description: Suburban commune directly north of Lyon, on the heights above the Saone with views over the city. Residential and green.
neighborhoods.character: Suburban, family-oriented, green spaces, quieter than central Lyon

housing.predominantType: Houses with gardens and apartment complexes with parking

neighborhoods.character_notes: Popular with families and retirees seeking quiet but city access. Metro C connects to central Lyon in 15 minutes.

neighborhoods.name: Villeurbanne (Gratte-Ciel)
neighborhoods.description: Adjacent city merged with Lyon's east, the Gratte-Ciel area is a 1930s Art Deco ensemble recently renovated with new amenities
neighborhoods.character: Urban, diverse, good transit, more affordable than central Lyon

housing.predominantType: Art Deco apartments and modern new-build residences

neighborhoods.character_notes: Major renovation completed 2023. TNP national theater is here. Metro A provides fast connection to Lyon center.
